steps.title: Find the time period from the exponent structure
steps.content:
The exponent is $3x$ where $x$ is measured in years. To find what time period the base 1.005 represents, we need the exponent to equal 1:
$$3x = 1 \implies x = \frac{1}{3} \text{ year}$$
Converting: $\frac{1}{3}$ year $= 4$ months. So the growth factor 1.005 applies to every 4-month interval.

steps.title: Interpret the growth factor 1.005
steps.content:
A balance multiplied by 1.005 can be rewritten as:
$$1.005 = 1 + 0.005 = 1 + 0.5\%$$
This means each new balance equals the old balance plus 0.5% of the old balance. In other words, every 4-month interval, the account balance increases by exactly 0.5% of its value at the start of that interval.

steps.title: Match to the correct choice
steps.content:
Evaluate each option:
  • A & C: Claim fixed dollar increases. This contradicts exponential growth, which produces percentage increases.
  • D: Claims 0.5% increase every 3 years. But at $x = 3$ years, the exponent is $3(3) = 9$, giving factor $(1.005)^9 \approx 1.045 \approx 4.5\%$ increase—not 0.5%.
  • B: At the end of every 4-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 4-month interval.
This is exactly what we derived above. That matches choice B — B is correct.
